business.headline: ICICI Bank hikes debit card DCC fee from 1% to 3.5%: What it meansDynamic Currency Conversion, commonly called DCC, allows customers to pay in their home currency instead of the local currency while making international transactions.

3 in 10 loan applications show ITR-bank mismatch: Can you still get a personal loan?Your ITR says one thing, but your bank statement tells a slightly different story — should you worry? As lenders tighten checks and nearly 3 in 10 loan applications show income mismatches, can you still get a personal loan without trouble? Let's find out here.

Why foreign investors are shunning India: Surjit Bhalla points out root causeEven as India's economy faces mounting pressure from rising crude oil prices and disruptions in fertiliser and energy supplies, economist and author Surjit Bhalla has identified a worsening investment climate as the country's biggest challenge.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as banking drag offsets IT rally; rupee hits record lowThe S&P BSE Sensex slipped 114.19 points, or 0.15%, to close at 75,200.85, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 31.95 points, or 0.14%, to settle at 23,618.

Govt likely to receive a big dividend from RBI in FY 27The Reserve Bank of India is expected to approve a record surplus transfer to the government this week. The payout could give the exchequer extra room as higher energy prices add fresh uncertainty.

Want to update Aadhaar for free? UIDAI has extended the deadline againThe UIDAI has announced that Aadhaar holders can continue updating documents linked to their Aadhaar card free of cost through the myAadhaar portal till June 14, 2027. The facility was earlier set to end in June 2026.

Rupee hits record low yet again, nears Rs 97 against US dollarThe rupee slipped to a fresh record low of 96.44 against the US dollar on Tuesday, nearing the Rs 97 mark. Rising crude prices, weak capital inflows and higher US yields are adding to pressure on India's external finances despite RBI intervention.

As milk, fuel and gold get costlier, here are 7 everyday expenses families may cutThe grocery bill feels heavier. Filling the petrol tank costs more. Even an occasional gold purchase suddenly seems expensive. Across India, rising prices are pinching household budgets, leaving many families asking: What everyday expenses can we cut without making life harder? Let's have a look.

Before you file your ITR this year, don't miss these details in AIS and Form 26ASAre you about to file your income tax return without checking AIS and Form 26AS? Before you hit submit, it may be worth taking a closer look, as even small mismatches in income or tax details can create problems later.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra shares jump: Why IT stocks gained up to 6% todayMajor IT stocks rebounded sharply on Tuesday, led by Infosys, TCS and midcap peers. The move reflected improving risk appetite, bargain hunting and renewed hopes of stronger US tech spending.

Explained: Why Adani Group stocks rose up to 5% todayAdani Group shares climbed in morning trade after the US moved to drop charges against Gautam Adani and his associates. The move, along with the LPG settlement, eased regulatory uncertainty and lifted investor confidence.

How the West Asia war triggered a global fight for India's oil marketRussia, Gulf producers and the United States are competing harder for India's oil market as the West Asia war disrupts trade routes. The scramble underlines India's growing leverage as one of the few big economies where oil demand is still rising.

Petrol, diesel prices rise again: Will 2022's 90-paise hike formula return?State-run oil companies have raised petrol and diesel prices again across major cities. The move has revived talk of a 2022-style pattern of small, frequent fuel hikes.

Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 23,700; Infosys up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 365.07 points, or 0.48%, to 75,680.11 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 107.55 points, or 0.45%, to 23,757.50, as of 9:40 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock market to crash after 2nd hike in fuel prices?Markets are set for a mildly positive open even after a second fuel price hike in a week. Hopes of easing tensions around Iran are offering support, but high crude prices and volatility are keeping traders cautious.

Standard Chartered plans 7,000 job cuts amid AI push and higher profit targetsStandard Chartered said it will cut more than 7,000 roles by 2030 as it leans on automation and artificial intelligence. The bank is tying the move to higher return targets and a bigger push in wealth management.

8th Pay Commission: The food-cost formula behind salary revision demandsEmployee unions have asked the 8th Pay Commission to recalculate minimum pay using updated food-cost and nutrition standards. The push shows how salary revision talks are increasingly centred on inflation, household budgets and daily living costs.

Petrol, diesel prices increased by 90 paise, second hike in 5 daysThe latest revision of prices follows a Rs 3-per-litre hike announced just days earlier, adding to the burden on consumers already facing high transport and household costs.

US drops fraud charges against Adani amid proposed $10 billion investment planThe move is being seen as the latest example of the Trump administration stepping away from high-profile prosecutions initiated during former President Joe Biden's administration.

India needs to triple its strategic petroleum reserves: Energy expert Anas AlhajjiIn an exclusive interview with India Today, energy expert Anas Alhajji spoke on the global oil crisis caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz due to the West Asia war and its impact on the economy and more

entertainment.headline: Twisha Sharma's co-actor Swetaa Varma reacts to her death, seeks fair probe Swetaa Varma has expressed shock over Twisha Sharma's death and called for a proper investigation. The case has widened after police booked Sharma's husband and mother-in-law and an SIT began its probe.

Kejriwal's dance to deleted photos: Singer-priest recalls Raghav-Parineeti wedding Vedic priest Ankit Batra shared new details from Parineeti Chopra and Raghav Chadha's Udaipur wedding. He revealed that Arvind Kejriwal danced in the baraat, while Madhu Chopra was asked to delete pictures from her phone.

Shikhar Pahariya cheers for Janhvi Kapoor after Peddi trailer, calls her 'lady don' Businessman Shikhar Pahariya reacted to the Peddi trailer and called girlfriend Janhvi Kapoor a 'lady don'. He also extended his love to the film's lead, Ram Charan.

Sonakshi Sinha, Jyotika on male stars being treated like 'God' on film sets Ahead of System's release, Sonakshi Sinha and Jyotika spoke to India Today about gender disparity on film sets, workplace bias and pay parity in the industry. The actors also described the film as a realistic courtroom and family drama centred on strong female characters.

Peddi director shuts down viral theory about Ram Charan losing a leg in film Ever since the Peddi trailer dropped, social media has been flooded with theories about Ram Charan's character suffering a shocking fate in the film. Director Buchi Babu Sana has now stepped in to address the speculation, while carefully holding back the real surprises.

Rishab Shetty reviews 'spectacular' Peddi trailer, calls Ram Charan absolute fire Rishab Shetty is all in for the Peddi trailer. Sharing the trailer on Instagram, the Kannada superstar showered love on Ram Charan, Shivarajkumar and other cast. The sports drama, directed by Buchi Babu Sana, will release globally on June 4.

Diljit Dosanjh says 'main bhi vaapas aaunga' after skipping trailer launch. Video Diljit Dosanjh missed the trailer launch of Main Vaapas Aaunga in Mumbai and addressed the audience through a video message. The upcoming film also stars Sharvari and Vedang Raina.

No one is coming to save you: Kangana to married women after Noida, Bhopal deaths Kangana Ranaut shared a note on the struggles faced by married women amid the Noida and Bhopal cases. She urged young women to prioritise financial independence before marriage.

Kamal Haasan skips luxury travel, takes economy flight to save costs for Kalki 2 Kamal Haasan has resumed shooting for the sequel to Kalki 2898 AD and travelled economy class to reach the set. The move was praised by Vyjayanthi Movies as it aligns with his recent call to cut avoidable production costs.

Actor Lawrence backs CM Vijay's decision to give film portfolio to MLA Rajmohan Actor-director Raghava Lawrence has defended Chief Minister Vijay's decision to give Egmore MLA Rajmohan the film technology and cinematograph portfolio. His remarks call for patience as Rajmohan takes charge amid rising concerns within the Tamil film industry.

Saif Ali Khan reveals why he shut down his successful production banner Actor Saif Ali Khan explained why he shut his production house, Illuminati Films. He said producing was creatively satisfying but often a thankless and demanding job.

New TV show Sairaab releases promo, internet screams 'Saiyaara vibes!' The upcoming show Sairaab has sparked comparisons with Saiyaara after its AI-generated promo went viral online. Viewers pointed to the similar visuals, while some also said the male lead resembled actor Ahaan Panday's character Krish Kapoor from the film.

Why Aamir Khan, Rajkumar Hirani changed PK's second half before release Actor Aamir Khan revealed that the second half of PK was rewritten before release. The actor said he and director Rajkumar Hirani were not fully satisfied with the final version. The film later became a major commercial success.

Shruti Haasan to turn writer for father Kamal Haasan's Hollywood film? Unconfirmed reports say Kamal Haasan may headline a Hollywood film scripted by Shruti Haasan. The buzz has intensified as he returns from politics to a crowded film slate and recent US visits.

Main Vaapas Aaunga trailer: Diljit Dosanjh brings a love torn apart by Partition The trailer of Main Vaapas Aaunga is out. The film, set in the Partition-era, is about a tale of love, memory and an enduring sense of home.

Daisy Shah recalls encounters with drunk fans at events: They're reeking of alcohol Daisy Shah recalled uncomfortable fan encounters at public events, including situations where people under the influence of alcohol made her uneasy.

Shivaleeka Oberoi, Abhishek Pathak name their daughter Aarika. Here's what it means Actor Shivaleeka Oberoi and her husband, director Abhishek Pathak, have introduced their daughter Aarika in a joint post. The announcement marked one month since her birth. They also revealed the meaning of her name.

Inside Khatron Ke Khiladi 15: Gaurav Khanna shares fun behind-the-scenes moments Actor Gaurav Khanna has begun sharing light moments with fellow contestants from the Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 shoot in Cape Town. The posts offer a playful break from the show's usual high-stakes image and are fuelling early curiosity around the new season.

Karuppu makers apologise, remove dialogue mocking Ilaiyaraaja's copyright fight The makers of Suriya's Karuppu have apologised for a dialogue referencing Ilaiyaraaja and promised to remove it. The move follows online criticism, even as the team says the scene was not meant to offend.

general news.headline: Popular or not, I won't let Iran blow up the world, says TrumpAnalysts believe Trump is attempting to wind down the conflict through a negotiated deal that would allow him to claim "victory" over Iran without losing political face. While strikes have largely ceased since the ceasefire in early April, normal shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted due to Iran's restrictions on the waterway and the ongoing US naval blockade.

'Not a spy': Who is the Norwegian journalist whose question to PM went viral?Norwegian journalist Helle Lyng tried to question Prime Minister Narendra Modi after a joint media briefing in Norway. The exchange and a later tense briefing by the MEA has turned attention to the little-known journalist and her social media activity.

Why TMC's 'Pushpa' Jahangir Khan bowed out of Falta repoll raceJahangir Khan withdrew from the Falta repoll days before voting. His exit has intensified focus on the TMC's silence and the politics around Abhishek Banerjee's turf.

Indian protocol officer found dead in room at High Commission in BangladeshPanic gripped the Indian mission in Bangladesh after a young protocol officer was found dead inside his room, prompting police intervention, an autopsy order and the launch of an unnatural death investigation.

Hai Nahin Ye Achchhi, Norwegian LadkiA joint media briefing by two heads of state is not a press conference for adversarial journalism. Norwegian Ladki Helle Lyng shouted a question at Prime Minister Modi, who is famous for not taking any. Helle has broken loose since.

Garib Nagar torn down as Mumbai rebuilds around BKC bullet train hubWestern Railway has launched one of Mumbai's biggest eviction drives at Bandra East's Garib Nagar to clear space for the Santacruz-Mumbai Central rail corridor expansion. The project aims to ease overcrowded local trains. It will also improve access to the upcoming Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train's Bandra-Kurla Complex (BKC) station.

Video: Rape accused garlanded, carried on shoulders after bail in UPSupporters welcomed rape accused Sushil Prajapati with garlands and slogans after his bail release in Ghaziabad. Viral videos of the procession have triggered outrage and a police inquiry.

China rejects report claiming Xi told Trump that Putin may regret Ukraine invasionChina has denied a report that Xi Jinping told Donald Trump Vladimir Putin might regret invading Ukraine. The denial came hours before Putin's Beijing meeting with Xi, underscoring the diplomatic sensitivity around their ties.

TCS employees say salary reduced after appraisal despite 5 percent hikeEmployees at TCS have raised concerns after some claimed their salaries had been reduced despite the company announcing average hikes of 5 per cent. Workers have blamed changes in CTC structure, lower variable pay, and revised appraisal calculations for the confusion.

Not India, China to be Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's first foreign destination?China could be the destination of Tarique Rahman's first foreign visit as the Prime Minister of Bangladesh, according to reports in Bangladeshi media. India too had invited Rahman for a visit, but he might choose Beijing over New Delhi for a state visit in late June.

How to spot AI writing? You cannot, not with AI checkers and not with your eyesWhat is common between a story in Granta, a viral blog piece on friendship, and a book published by Penguin? If you have spent some time on social media in the last few days, you will realise that all three have been deemed work of AI. Also, probably erroneously.

Why Rishabh Pant was removed as India's Test vice-captainAjit Agarkar said Rishabh Pant remains central to India's Test plans despite losing the vice-captaincy and his ODI place. The selectors have split his role for now, with KL Rahul elevated and other wicketkeeping options preferred in one-day cricket.

Virat Kohli reveals impostor syndrome, says Rahul Dravid took care of him mentallyFormer India captain Virat Kohli has opened up about battling "impostor syndrome" during his career, revealing that former head coach Rahul Dravid and batting coach Vikram Rathour played a major role in helping him rediscover the joy of playing cricket after stepping down from captaincy.

Rohit Sharma picked subject to fitness, Ishan Kishan back in ODI squadIndia vs Afghanistan: The BCCI announced a 15-member squad, led by Shubman Gill. While Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya have been named despite fitness concerns, Rishabh Pant has lost his place.

The journalist is not the story! A shout is not a question!Norwegian journalist Helle Lyng openly admitted on social media that she never expected PM Modi to answer her shouted query. She knew the protocol. The narrative ceased to be about India's press freedom landscape. It became entirely about her.

After The Kashmir Files and The Bengal Files, now comes The Maharashtra FilesThe Maharashtra Files trailer sets up a tense battle between farmers and the powerful people. The Marathi political thriller releases in theatres on May 29, 2026.

Kejriwal's dance to deleted photos: Singer-priest recalls Raghav-Parineeti weddingVedic priest Ankit Batra shared new details from Parineeti Chopra and Raghav Chadha's Udaipur wedding. He revealed that Arvind Kejriwal danced in the baraat, while Madhu Chopra was asked to delete pictures from her phone.

Delhi registers hottest May day in 2 years as temperature hits 45.1°CAmong several stations, Ridge recorded the maximum temperature at 46.5°C, making it the hottest location in Delhi during the day. Safdarjung and Palam registered 45.1°C, while Lodhi Road recorded 45.2°C and Ayanagar touched 45.5°C.

Why foreign investors are shunning India: Surjit Bhalla points out root causeEven as India's economy faces mounting pressure from rising crude oil prices and disruptions in fertiliser and energy supplies, economist and author Surjit Bhalla has identified a worsening investment climate as the country's biggest challenge.

Video: 9 people trapped in lift at Karnataka mall, hour-long rescue operation followsNine people were rescued after a lift malfunctioned at a mall in Karnataka on Sunday night. The hour-long operation has renewed focus on lift maintenance and emergency readiness in crowded commercial spaces.

Woman walks into Karnataka temple with shoes on, tourist lashes out at herA now-viral video from a historic Badami temple showed a tourist confronting a woman allegedly wearing footwear inside. The episode has sparked debate over religious decorum, public conduct and online reactions.

Prabhakaran, Tamil identity and TVK: Is Vijay building a neo-Dravidian project?Repeatedly invoking the memory of Prabhakaran and the emotional legacy of the Tamil cause, Vijay appears to be crafting a new ideological synthesis of the welfare-driven legacy of Dravidianism with the cultural assertiveness of Tamil nationalism.

Pak officer claims missiles struck 2 Indian airbases. They don't even existWhile Rajouri is a district in Jammu and Kashmir and does not host any operational Indian Air Force airbase. Mamun, on the other hand, is a military cantonment area near Pathankot, Punjab, not an airbase. Indian users were quick to point out the misinformation and mock the officer for "striking" non-existent military bases.

Fake news, says BSF, as Bangladesh outlets report on exchange of fire at borderSeveral Bangladeshi media outlets reported that the Border Guard Bangladesh (BGB) and India's Border Security Force exchanged fire at a border area in Meghalaya. A BSF source denied any firing incident and told India Today Digital that it was "fake news".

sports.headline: Struggling Carlos Alcaraz pulls out of Wimbledon after missing French Open World No. 2 Carlos Alcaraz confirmed that he will miss the upcoming Wimbledon Championships, scheduled to begin in June, as he continues his recovery from a wrist injury. The Spaniard announced his withdrawal from this year's tournament due to the lingering issue.

Virat Kohli urges next generation to chase greatness beyond instant IPL fame Star Indian batter Virat Kohli urged young cricketers not to get carried away by the instant fame and financial rewards of the IPL, saying the real challenge lies in staying committed to the sport for a long period and earning respect across the cricketing world.

Are IPL teams forcing injured stars to play? BCCI secretary breaks silence IPL 2026: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia said franchises make the final call on injured players during the IPL. His remarks followed scrutiny over KKR playing Varun Chakravarthy despite a hairline fracture.

England Women's T20 World Cup scare: Sciver-Brunt ruled out of warm-up series Nat Sciver-Brunt will miss England's T20 series against New Zealand and India after a calf tear. The injury disrupts a crucial World Cup build-up and forces further changes to England's squad.

Never back down: PV Sindhu reveals priceless advice from Virat Kohli on special meet India's badminton star PV Sindhu revealed the inspiring advice she received from Virat Kohli during their recent meeting in Bengaluru, praising the RCB star for constantly guiding and motivating fellow athletes.

Big blow for RR as injured Riyan Parag misses LSG clash IPL 2026: Riyan Parag missed Rajasthan Royals' clash against Lucknow Super Giants with a hamstring injury. His absence, along with Ravindra Jadeja's continued recovery, has added to RR's playoff worries.

Why Rishabh Pant was removed as India's Test vice-captain Ajit Agarkar said Rishabh Pant remains central to India's Test plans despite losing the vice-captaincy and his ODI place. The selectors have split his role for now, with KL Rahul elevated and other wicketkeeping options preferred in one-day cricket.

Virat Kohli reveals impostor syndrome, says Rahul Dravid took care of him mentally Former India captain Virat Kohli has opened up about battling "impostor syndrome" during his career, revealing that former head coach Rahul Dravid and batting coach Vikram Rathour played a major role in helping him rediscover the joy of playing cricket after stepping down from captaincy.

Cristiano Ronaldo set for record sixth FIFA World Cup as Portugal announce squad Cristiano Ronaldo has been picked in Portugal's 27-man squad for what would be his sixth World Cup. The call keeps his chase for a first world title alive and adds an emotional Diogo Jota tribute.

RR vs LSG LIVE: Jaiswal, Sooryavanshi make flying start to 221 chase RR (Rajasthan) vs LSG (Lucknow) Live Score, IPL 2026: RR (19/0 in 0.5 ovs). Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi have made a flying start as the Royals are looking to chase down 221.

Total shame: Auqib Nabi omission from Afghanistan Test leaves fans furious IND vs AFG: Auqib Nabi's omission from India's Afghanistan Test squad sparked outrage among fans despite the Jammu and Kashmir pacer enjoying a sensational domestic season.

Days after getting Virat Kohli on a duck, Prince Yadav earns maiden ODI call-upPrince Yadav has earned a first India ODI call-up after a strong domestic and IPL run. This comes amid Virat Kohli's praise of the young pacer and India's rotation call underlining a shift towards testing new pace depth.

Has India moved on from Mohammed Shami? Agarkar casts doubt on pacer's returnAjit Agarkar said Mohammed Shami was not considered for India's longer-format squads against Afghanistan. The remark has sharpened questions over Shami's place in India's ODI and Test plans.

Malaysia Masters 2026: Arjun, Hariharan avenge Thomas Cup loss to Popov brothersHariharan Amsakarunan and M R Arjun beat Christo and Toma Junior Popov in straight games at the Malaysia Masters. The win offered a sharp response after India's Thomas Cup semi-final loss to France earlier this month.

Who is Gurnoor Brar? 6ft 3in Punjab pacer earns maiden India Test call-upPunjab pacer Gurnoor Brar has earned his maiden India Test call-up for the one-off match against Afghanistan next month following a string of impressive performances in domestic cricket and the IPL. The right-arm fast bowler's consistent rise over the last two seasons has now brought him to the doorstep of an international debut.

Rohit Sharma picked subject to fitness, Ishan Kishan back in ODI squadIndia vs Afghanistan: The BCCI announced a 15-member squad, led by Shubman Gill. While Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya have been named despite fitness concerns, Rishabh Pant has lost his place.

KL Rahul replaces Rishabh Pant as vice-captain as BCCI announces Test squadThe BCCI on Tuesday announced India's squad for the one-off Test against Afghanistan, with KL Rahul replacing Rishabh Pant as the vice-captain of the Shubman Gill-led side.

How RCB avoided title hangover in IPL 2026: Mo Bobat explains the hunter mindsetMo Bobat said RCB adopted a hunter mindset to reset after last season's title win. He said that approach has helped the side stay driven through a strong IPL 2026 campaign.

MI hit with double blow as star batter, all-rounder ruled out of IPL 2026Mumbai Indians have ruled out Quinton de Kock and Raj Angad Bawa for the rest of IPL 2026 due to injury setbacks. Already out of playoff contention, MI will finish their campaign with matches against Kolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals.

Ashwin to angry CSK fans: Enjoy Ishan Kishan celebration, why bring ego into it?IPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin asked CSK fans not to take Ishan Kishan's Chepauk celebration personally after SRH's win. He said sport needs emotion and drama, though such exchanges should stay respectful.

India squad in good shape for Women's T20 World Cup: Smriti MandhanaVice-captain Smriti Mandhana on Tuesday avoided revealing too much about the Indian women's team's preparations for next month's T20 World Cup, but said the squad is in good condition ahead of the 12-team event.

Ruturaj will be a fine captain: Fleming backs CSK skipper amid transition periodChennai Super Kings head coach Stephen Fleming admitted Ruturaj Gaikwad is still learning as a captain after the team's IPL playoff hopes took a major hit with the defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad.

Harbouring Olympic dreams, Puja Tomar eyes impact beyond UFC octagon for IndiaIndia's UFC trailblazer Puja Tomar opens up on her growth as a fighter, India's untapped MMA potential, her dream of launching an academy, and why representing the country at the Olympics would be the ultimate honour.

RR vs LSG: Can rattled Royals settle down and push for playoffs?RR vs LSG Preview: Rajasthan Royals head back home in Jaipur to play their penultimate league game of the season. Can they keep their heads and make a final push for the playoffs this season?

IND v AFG selection live: Rohit picked in ODI squad, Pant loses Test vice-captaincyIndia (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) ODI, one-off Test squad announcement live updates: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI)'s Selection committee have come together to get the meeting underway for the one-off Test and three-match ODI series in Afghanistan is now underway.

MS Dhoni might not have played in 2026, but he's still important for CSK: FlemingChennai Super Kings head coach Stephen Fleming defended MS Dhoni's time away from the game in IPL 2026, while Suresh Raina revealed he is actively pressing the franchise legend for a 2027 comeback.

IND vs AFG: Rohit Sharma, Hardik Pandya's availability hinges on fitness clearanceRohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya are awaiting medical clearance before the Afghanistan ODI series. Their fitness status has left India's team management seeking clarity on two senior players.

Big Bash League set for historic debut in India, BCCI gives final green lightChennai will host a Big Bash League match. The fixture, after months of talks, signals Cricket Australia's push into India with the BCCI's approval.

Hardik Pandya trains at full intensity in Kolkata. Is a return on cards?KKR vs MI: Hardik Pandya trained at full intensity ahead of MI's IPL match against KKR on Wednesday. Returning from a back spasm, the skipper bowled at full tilt amid rumours, even as MI stands knocked out of the playoff race.

Pep Guardiola to leave Manchester City, ex-Chelsea boss expected to take overPep Guardiola is reportedly set to leave Manchester City, ending a trophy-laden era at the Etihad, with former assistant Enzo Maresca emerging as the leading candidate to replace him.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
